2603.16495.title: ExpressMind: A Multimodal Pretrained Large Language Model for Expressway Operation
2603.16495.paper_summary: The current expressway operation relies on rule-based and isolated models, which limits the ability to jointly analyze knowledge across different systems. Meanwhile, Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly applied in intelligent transportation, advancing traffic models from algorithmic to cognitive intelligence. However, general LLMs are unable to effectively understand the regulations and causal relationships of events in unconventional scenarios in the expressway field. Therefore, this paper constructs a pre-trained multimodal large language model (MLLM) for expressways, ExpressMind, which serves as the cognitive core for intelligent expressway operations. This paper constructs the industry's first full-stack expressway dataset, encompassing traffic knowledge texts, emergency reasoning chains, and annotated video events to overcome data scarcity. This paper proposes a dual-layer LLM pre-training paradigm based on self-supervised training and unsupervised learning. Additionally, this study introduces a Graph-Augmented RAG framework to dynamically index the expressway knowledge base. To enhance reasoning for expressway incident response strategies, we develop a RL-aligned Chain-of-Thought (RL-CoT) mechanism that enforces consistency between model reasoning and expert problem-solving heuristics for incident handling. Finally, ExpressMind integrates a cross-modal encoder to align the dynamic feature sequences under the visual and textual channels, enabling it to understand traffic scenes in both video and image modalities. Extensive experiments on our newly released multi-modal expressway benchmark demonstrate that ExpressMind comprehensively outperforms existing baselines in event detection, safety response generation, and complex traffic analysis. The code and data are available at: https://wanderhee.github.io/ExpressMind/.
2603.16495.paper_summary_zh: 目前的高速公路運營依賴於基於規則和孤立的模型，這限制了跨不同系統共同分析知識的能力。<lb/>同時，大型語言模型（LLMs）在智能交通領域的應用日益增多，將交通模型從算法智能推進到認知智能。<lb/>然而，通用的LLMs無法有效理解高速公路領域中非常規場景的規則和事件的因果關係。<lb/>因此，本文構建了一個針對高速公路的預訓練多模態大型語言模型（MLLM），名為ExpressMind，作為智能高速公路運營的認知核心。<lb/>本文構建了行業首個全棧高速公路數據集，涵蓋交通知識文本、應急推理鏈和標註視頻事件，以克服數據稀缺問題。<lb/>本文提出了一種基於自我監督訓練和無監督學習的雙層LLM預訓練範式。<lb/>此外，本研究引入了一個圖增強的RAG框架，以動態索引高速公路知識庫。<lb/>為了增強高速公路事件響應策略的推理能力，我們開發了一個與強化學習對齊的思維鏈（RL-CoT）機制，強化模型推理與專家問題解決啟發式之間的一致性。<lb/>最後，ExpressMind整合了一個跨模態編碼器，以對齊視覺和文本通道下的動態特徵序列，使其能夠理解視頻和圖像模態中的交通場景。<lb/>在我們新發布的多模態高速公路基準上進行的廣泛實驗表明，ExpressMind在事件檢測、安全響應生成和複雜交通分析方面全面超越了現有基準。<lb/>代碼和數據可在以下鏈接獲得：https://wanderhee.github.io/ExpressMind/.
2603.16495.author: Zihe Wang et.al.
2603.16495.authors: Zihe Wang, Yihuan Wang, Haiyang Yu. Zhiyong Cui, Xiaojian Liao, Chengcheng Wang, Yonglin Tian, Yongxin Tong
